rulings.Case Title: JUNE AURE VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, April 7, 2015, Line 1, DEFENDANT EMERSON POWER TRANSMISSION CORPORATION's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication. The court grants defendant's motion for summary judgment based on the component parts doctrine. The answer sufficiently raises that issue. Defendant timely filed and served the motion. Defendant's notice of motion indicates that defendant personally served the motion on January 21, 2015, and provided the required 75-days notice based on the April 7, 2015, hearing date. See CCP 437c. The court issued an order allowing the motion to be heard within 30 days prior to trial. Plaintiff concedes most of defendant's facts and it is undisputed that defendant's bushings and gear reducers are incorporated into a wide variety of products. (UMF 1). Defendant's products are sold to entities without any specification regarding their application. (UMF 4). Defendant had no role in designing, assembling, installing, or maintaining the BAE baggage system or how its parts would be installed. (UMF 17 and 18). Given the above, the components parts doctrine applies to this case. That doctrine "rests on 'a line of cases holding an entity supplying a nondefective raw material or a component part is not strictly liable for defects in the final product over which it had no control.'" Bay Summit Community Assn. v. Shell Oil Co. (1996) 51 Cal.App.4th 762, 772. Plaintiff does not highlight a specific defect with defendant's products and the duty to provide a guard fell on BAE. Tellez-Cordova v. Cambell-Hausfeld/Scott Fetzger Co., (2004) 129 Cal.App.4th 577 is distinguishable. There, the court held on appeal from dismissal on demurrer that a strict liability claim against a manufacturer of grinding tools was viable. The court ruled that the grinder was specifically designed to be used with toxic products and toxic dust was released when using the grinder in a foreseeable manner. That same specificity is lacking in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CCP 437c(g) with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: CHRIS MIORELLI et al VS. WYNDHAM VACATION OWNERSHIP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday April 7, 2015, Line 2, PLAINTIFF CHRIS MIORELLI, TRINA USA LLC'S Motion For Reconsideration. Pro Tem Judge Noah Lebowitz, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Continued on the court's own motion to April 21, 2015. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to noah@dplolaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: CAPITAL ONE BANK (USA), N.A. VS. SASHA R PENOWICH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Terminating Sanctions, And In The Alternative, To Further Compel Response To Discovery
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday April 7, 2015, Line 3, PLAINTIFF CAPITAL ONE BANK (USA), N.A.'S Motion Terminating Sanctions, And In The Alternative, To Further Compel Response To Discovery. Pro Tem Judge Noah Lebowitz, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's motion is DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE to refiling. No opposition was filed, however, the plaintiff's declaration was insufficient to support the request for terminating sanctions. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to noah@dplolaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. rulings.Case Title: HUI ZHEN LIANG VS. FOOD EXPRESS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, April 7, 2015, Line 11, DEFENDANT COMMUS LLC DBA FOOD EXPRESS' DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The Demurrer is granted in part and overruled in part. The Demurrer is granted without leave to amend as to the first and second causes of action. The one-year statute of limitations for libel and slander (Code Civ. Proc., §§ 335, 340, subd. 3) is applicable to claims for invasion of privacy. [See Weiner v. Superior Court, (1976) 58 Cal. App. 3d 525.] The Demurrer is overruled as to the fourth cause of action. The FAC adequately sets forth the ultimate facts necessary to establish each element of the cause of action. (See FAC at 25-27.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
